metadata.title: States PCS: Bihar GK for BPSC and other Bihar PCS Exams

questions.question: Arya, the author of the book ‘Field Facilitator Guide’ related to rural entrepreneurship, is related to which district of Bihar?
questions.explanation: Arya, the author of the book ‘Field Facilitator Guide’ belongs to the Muzaffarpur district. This book demonstrates how to break down barriers and support female and rural businesses. It took one and a half years to complete. It has been released by the Department of Rural Development, and the ministry will start planning to utilize the methods suggested in it.

questions.question: Recently Narendra Singh passed away, he is related to which district of Bihar?
questions.explanation: Narendra Singh, a former minister of agriculture for Bihar and a member of the JP movement, passed away in a Patna private hospital after a protracted illness at the age of 75. He is related to the Jamui district of Bihar.

questions.question: Bihar’s first ‘Modi-Nitish Nagar’ will be built in which district?
questions.explanation: The townships will first be built at Rajaun in the Banka district. Many individuals lack the land necessary to build homes; therefore, under this program, the land will be allocated to them and supported under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana.

questions.question: Recently, the proposal to give a GI tag to the stone craft of Patharkatti has been approved, it is related to which district of Bihar?
questions.explanation: the proposal to give a GI tag to the stone craft of Patharkatti has been approved, it is related to the Gaya district of Bihar. The Geographical Indications of Goods(Registration and Protection) Act, 1999 protects goods and objects with GI tags by allowing the owner of the indication to forbid a third party from using it.

questions.question: Consider the following pairs: Zardalu Mango – Maharashtra Mircha Rice – Assam Shahi Litchi – Bihar Magahi Paan – Bihar Which of the pairs given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Mircha rice of Bihar’s West Champaran gets GI tag. The size and shape of the grain appear like that of black pepper, hence it is known as Mircha or Marcha Rice. The grains and flakes of this rice have a unique aroma that makes it different. Other GI products from Bihar –Zardalu mango, Katarni rice of Bhagalpur, Shahi litchi of Muzaffarpur, Magahi paan of Magadh region, and Makhana of Mithila.

questions.question: Recently, researchers have identified anti-diabetic herb ‘Gymnema sylvestre’ in which hills of Bihar?
questions.explanation: Researchers identified Gymnema sylvestre (Gurmar) on Brahmayoni Hill in Gaya, Bihar, known for its diabetes treatment potential. Gurmar, used in the anti-diabetic drug BGR-34 by CSIR, reduces blood sugar by blocking intestinal sugar absorption. It contains gymnemic acid, flavonoids, and saponins, which regulate lipid metabolism and lower cholesterol. The study emphasizes the importance of Gurmar in managing diabetes, alongside other medicinal plants. Researchers call for more in-depth studies on Gurmar and advocate for local cultivation to ensure its sustainability and prevent extinction.

questions.question: Who from Bihar was honored with the prestigious Distinguished Philatelist Award (RDP) by the Royal Philatelic Society of London in 2023?
questions.explanation: Pradeep Jain, a renowned philatelist from Patna, Bihar was honored with the prestigious Distinguished Philatelist Award (RDP) by the Royal Philatelic Society of London in 2023. This is a significant recognition, as only two other Indians have received this award since independence. Jain has made notable contributions to the world of philately and joins an elite group of awardees recognized for their expertise in stamp collecting.

questions.question: Under Pradhan Mantri Micro Food Industry Upgradation Scheme, Bihar government has initiated a plan to establish food processing units in all __?
questions.explanation: The Bihar government has initiated a plan to establish food processing units in all 8,387 panchayats under the Pradhan Mantri Micro Food Industry Upgradation Scheme. The scheme aims to utilize agro-based products, promote self-employment, and reduce migration by creating jobs locally. The government provides loans to applicants to start new industries and offers a 35% credit-linked capital subsidy for upgrading existing units, with a maximum cap of Rs 10 lakh per unit.

questions.question: Which was the first Vande Bharat express train to operate in Bihar?
questions.explanation: The first Vande Bharat Express train to operate in Bihar was the Patna–Ranchi Vande Bharat Express. This train began service in 2024 and took approximately 6 hours to cover a distance of 379 km from Patna to Ranchi. The train had a maximum permissible speed of 130 km/h and ran 6 days a week.

questions.question: In terms of lightning-related deaths, which three districts in Bihar are the most vulnerable?
questions.explanation: A recent study of lightning-related deaths in Bihar showed that Sheohar, Banka and Kaimur districts were the most vulnerable to this natural hazard, recording the highest casualty rate per million population. The study, which examined data from 2017-2022, found that 1,624 people died and 286 were injured due to lightning, with nearly all occurring in rural areas. Most casualties and injuries, about 76.8 per cent, happened between 12:30 pm and 6:30 pm.
